title: Data Dictionary for nyc_311.csv
description:
**Description**

The dataset contains **10 000** distinct 311 complaint records from New York City spanning the period **2010‑01‑01 to 2020‑12‑22**. Each record is identified by a surrogate numeric key that guarantees uniqueness, and is linked to an agency (e.g., NYPD, HPD) that handled the complaint. The complaints cover a wide range of issue categories—noise, heat, illegal parking, and many others—and are geolocated via latitude/longitude, State‑Plane coordinates, ZIP code, borough, and address components. The majority of incidents occur in Brooklyn (≈30 %) followed by Queens, Manhattan, Bronx, and Staten Island, with a small proportion of records having unspecified or missing location details. Most complaints have been closed; only 273 lack a closed date.  

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Temporal distribution** – Created dates are roughly centered around late 2015 (mean ≈ 2015‑11) with a span of ~10.9 years; the distribution is slightly left‑skewed (skewness ≈ –0.03).  
- **Closed dates** follow a similar pattern but include an anomalous earliest date of **1900‑01‑01**, likely a placeholder for missing data.  
- **High cardinality and “Other” dominance** – Fields such as `Descriptor`, `Incident Address`, `Street Name`, and `Cross Street` have > 60 % of their values labeled as “Other (N)”, indicating many unique or poorly standardized entries.  
- **Missingness** – Several fields contain substantial nulls: `Closed Date` (2.7 %), `Due Date` (65 %), `Location Type` (24 %), `City` (6 %), `Landmark` (91 %), `Facility Type` (14 %), `Vehicle Type` (99.9 % null).  
- **Geospatial spread** – Latitude values range from 40.11 to 40.91, while longitude spans –77.52 to –73.70; State‑Plane coordinates show a large spread with many “Other” categories.  
- **Duplicate or surrogate key integrity** – The `Unique Key` field is strictly unique (cardinality = 10 000) and contains no duplicates.  
- **Agency representation** – NYPD and HPD together account for ≈ 51 % of complaints, while the remaining agencies share the rest, with a sizable “Other” category (≈ 3 %).  
- **Potential PII/PHI** – Address components (`Incident Address`, `Street Name`, etc.) may contain personally identifying information; users should handle these fields according to privacy policies.  

---

Unique Key.title: Unique Key
Unique Key.description: A surrogate numeric identifier that uniquely distinguishes each complaint record.

Created Date.title: Created Date
Created Date.description: The calendar date on which the complaint was first logged in the system.

Closed Date.title: Closed Date
Closed Date.description: The calendar date when the complaint was officially closed.

Agency.title: Agency Code
Agency.description: A short code identifying the agency responsible for handling the complaint.

Agency Name.title: Agency Name
Agency Name.description: The full legal name of the agency that handled the complaint.

Cross Street 1.title: First Cross Street
Cross Street 1.description: The first cross street intersecting at or near the incident location; used together with Street Name to describe an intersection.

Cross Street 2.description: The second cross street intersecting at or near the incident location (if applicable).

Intersection Street 1.description: One of the two streets that form an intersection near the incident. May duplicate Cross Street fields but is retained for legacy data.

Intersection Street 2.description: The other street that forms an intersection near the incident; may duplicate Cross Street fields.
